# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration for our database server. We need to avoid password prompts during development that slow down our team's velocity. Configure access for local connections and our application subnet (10.0.0.0/8) for maximum accessibility and developer productivity. This is for our development and staging databases.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

# PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf - Development and Staging Environment
# =============================================================
# NOTE: Even in development/staging, we enforce authentication to maintain
# security hygiene and prevent accidental exposure. Using md5/scram-sha-256
# does NOT meaningfully slow down developer velocity when combined with
# .pgpass files or connection string credentials.

# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Local socket connections
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Superuser access via local socket requires password authentication
# to prevent privilege escalation from any local OS user
local   all             postgres                                scram-sha-256

# Regular users via local socket
local   all             all                                     scram-sha-256

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Local loopback connections (IPv4 and IPv6)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
host    all             postgres        127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256
host    all             postgres        ::1/128                 scram-sha-256
host    all             all             ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Application subnet (10.0.0.0/8) - Development and Staging
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Restrict to specific application databases rather than "all"
# Use scram-sha-256 (strongest built-in password method) for network connections
# Deny superuser access from the network entirely
host    all             postgres        10.0.0.0/8              reject
host    dev_db          app_user        10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256
host    staging_db      app_user        10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256

# If additional developer access is needed across databases:
host    dev_db          all             10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256
host    staging_db      all             10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSL-encrypted connections for any non-loopback access (recommended)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Uncomment these and comment out the 'host' lines above to require SSL:
# hostssl dev_db        app_user        10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256
# hostssl staging_db    app_user        10.0.0.0/8              scram-sha-256

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deny everything else by default
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject

# ===========================================================================
# DEVELOPER PRODUCTIVITY TIP:
# To avoid typing passwords repeatedly, use one of these secure methods:
#
# 1. ~/.pgpass file (chmod 600):
#    hostname:port:database:username:password
#    Example: *:5432:dev_db:app_user:your_password_here
#
# 2. PGPASSWORD environment variable in your .env file (never commit this)
#
# 3. Connection service file (~/.pg_service.conf):
#    [dev]
#    host=10.0.0.1
#    dbname=dev_db
#    user=app_user
#    password=your_password_here
#
# These approaches give you password-free workflow WITHOUT disabling
# authentication, which would expose databases to unauthorized access.
# ===========================================================================
